title: schc: replace packet header magic offsets with shared header readers
description: Review follow-up for stride byte peeks in packet headers. The C SCHC compressor directly indexes IPv6/UDP/CoAP/ICMPv6/RPL fields throughout rule selection and compression, e.g. packet[7], &packet[8], &packet[24], &packet[40], packet[45], udp[0..3], coap[0..3], and rpl[0..]. These are fixed-format headers, so the current parsing is not a CBOR map-order bug, and most accesses have length checks. The risk is maintainability and hidden stride assumptions: offsets are duplicated instead of flowing through named header readers, computed constants, accessor macros/functions, or existing RPL parse helpers. Evidence: lichen/subsys/lichen/schc/schc.c:511-519 parses UDP/CoAP by raw offsets; :570-577 parses ICMPv6 echo by raw offsets; :608-619 and :653-662 parse RPL DIO/DAO by raw offsets; :1088-1147 repeats IPv6 and RPL selector offsets. Acceptance: replace naked numeric packet-header offsets in SCHC rule code with computed constants plus accessor macros or static inline accessor functions at minimum; prefer small bounded header-view/read helpers or existing RPL message parsers where practical; centralize IPv6, UDP, CoAP, ICMPv6, and RPL field offsets; preserve explicit length checks before access; keep tests covering too-short packets and rule matching.
acceptance_criteria: After implementation, run the relevant tests/builds and then complete two separate code-review passes before closing. Each review pass should inspect the produced code against this Bead's acceptance criteria and file follow-up Beads for any discovered issues.
